title: hydrophyllum virginianum var. patens
intro: Hydrophyllum virginianum var. patens, also known as American waterleaf, is a perennial plant native to eastern North America that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of American waterleaf include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Hydrophyllum virginianum var. patens can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and its leaves can be added to salads or used as a culinary herb, although its use as a food should be done with caution due to possible toxicity.
